note: Hawaii has no traditional truck stops on its H-route interstates (H-1, H-2, H-3, all on Oahu). Routes are short urban/military corridors with no travel centers, truck-stop chains, or rest areas with overnight truck parking. Commercial fueling is handled at cardlock/harbor-area facilities off the interstates (e.g., near Honolulu Harbor and Campbell Industrial Park). There are also no permanent weigh stations or ports of entry on the H-route interstates; commercial vehicle weighing is done at harbor and portable enforcement sites.
